title: Nassella nidulans
taxonomy: Nassella nidulans is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. Nassella nidulans is native to the Americas and is often found in dry to moist areas. Nassella nidulans is classified as a perennial grass that grows in clumps. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Stipeae. Nassella nidulans is also known by its synonym, Stipa nidulans.
uses: Nassella nidulans is known for its traditional uses in medicine, particularly in South America. The plant has been employed in the treatment of various health issues, including digestive problems and fever. Its leaves and roots are used to prepare infusions and decoctions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Nassella nidulans is also used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as coughs and colds. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Nassella nidulans for medicinal purposes.
